97	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
98	# Helpers
99	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
100	
101	def _serialize_blacklist(entry):
102	    return {
103	        "id": entry.id,
104	        "target_type": entry.target_type,
105	        "target_id": entry.target_id,
106	        "reason": entry.reason,
107	        "start_at": entry.start_at.isoformat() if entry.start_at else None,
108	        "end_at": entry.end_at.isoformat() if entry.end_at else None,
109	        "appeal_status": entry.appeal_status,
110	        "created_at": entry.created_at.isoformat() if entry.created_at else None,
111	    }
112	
113	
114	def _serialize_risk_event(event):
115	    reasons = event.reasons
116	    if reasons:
117	        try:
118	            reasons = json.loads(reasons)
119	        except (ValueError, TypeError):
120	            pass
121	    return {
122	        "id": event.id,
123	        "event_type": event.event_type,
124	        "user_id": event.user_id,
125	        "ip": event.ip,
126	        "device_id": event.device_id,
127	        "decision": event.decision,
128	        "reasons": reasons,
129	        "metadata_json": event.metadata_json,
130	        "created_at": event.created_at.isoformat() if event.created_at else None,
131	    }
132	
133	
134	def _check_ip_blacklist(ip):
135	    """Return True if the given IP is actively blacklisted."""
136	    if not ip:
137	        return False
138	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
139	    entry = (
140	        db.session.query(__import__("app.models.risk", fromlist=["Blacklist"]).Blacklist)
141	        .filter(
142	            __import__("app.models.risk", fromlist=["Blacklist"]).Blacklist.target_type == "ip",
143	            __import__("app.models.risk", fromlist=["Blacklist"]).Blacklist.target_id == ip,
144	            __import__("app.models.risk", fromlist=["Blacklist"]).Blacklist.start_at <= now,
145	            (
146	                __import__("app.models.risk", fromlist=["Blacklist"]).Blacklist.end_at == None  # noqa: E711
147	            ) | (
148	                __import__("app.models.risk", fromlist=["Blacklist"]).Blacklist.end_at > now
149	            ),
150	        )
151	        .first()
152	    )
153	    return entry is not None
154

160	@blp.route("/evaluate")
161	class EvaluateView(MethodView):
162	    @blp.doc(
163	        summary="Evaluate risk for an event",
164	        description="Submit an event for risk evaluation. Returns decision and triggered signals.",
165	        security=[{"BearerAuth": []}],
166	    )
167	    @blp.arguments(EvaluateRequestSchema)
168	    @blp.response(200, EvaluateResponseSchema)
169	    @require_auth
170	    def post(self, data):
171	        from app.services.risk_service import evaluate_risk
172	
173	        ip = data.get("ip") or request.remote_addr or "unknown"
174	
175	        # Check IP blacklist before evaluation
176	        from app.models.risk import Blacklist
177	        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
178	        ip_blacklisted = (
179	            Blacklist.query
180	            .filter(
181	                Blacklist.target_type == "ip",
182	                Blacklist.target_id == ip,
183	                Blacklist.start_at <= now,
184	                (Blacklist.end_at == None) | (Blacklist.end_at > now),  # noqa: E711
185	            )
186	            .first()
187	        )
188	        if ip_blacklisted:
189	            return jsonify({"error": "forbidden", "message": "IP address is blacklisted"}), 403
190	
191	        result = evaluate_risk(
192	            event_type=data["event_type"],
193	            ip=ip,
194	            user_id=data.get("user_id"),
195	            device_id=data.get("device_id"),
196	            metadata=data.get("metadata"),
197	        )
198	        return result
199

201	@blp.route("/events")
202	class RiskEventsView(MethodView):
203	    @blp.doc(
204	        summary="List risk events (admin only)",
205	        description="Paginated, filterable list of risk events.",
206	        security=[{"BearerAuth": []}],
207	    )
208	    @blp.arguments(RiskEventsQuerySchema, location="query")
209	    @require_auth
210	    @require_role("admin")
211	    def get(self, query):
212	        from app.models.risk import RiskEvent
213	
214	        q = db.session.query(RiskEvent)
215	
216	        if query.get("user_id"):
217	            q = q.filter(RiskEvent.user_id == query["user_id"])
218	        if query.get("ip"):
219	            q = q.filter(RiskEvent.ip == query["ip"])
220	        if query.get("event_type"):
221	            q = q.filter(RiskEvent.event_type == query["event_type"])
222	        if query.get("decision"):
223	            q = q.filter(RiskEvent.decision == query["decision"])
224	        if query.get("date_from"):
225	            try:
226	                dt = datetime.fromisoformat(query["date_from"])
227	                q = q.filter(RiskEvent.created_at >= dt)
228	            except ValueError:
229	                pass
230	        if query.get("date_to"):
231	            try:
232	                dt = datetime.fromisoformat(query["date_to"])
233	                q = q.filter(RiskEvent.created_at <= dt)
234	            except ValueError:
235	                pass
236	
237	        page = max(1, query.get("page", 1))
238	        per_page = min(100, max(1, query.get("per_page", 20)))
239	        total = q.count()
240	        events = q.order_by(RiskEvent.created_at.desc()).offset((page - 1) * per_page).limit(per_page).all()
241	
242	        return jsonify({
243	            "total": total,
244	            "page": page,
245	            "per_page": per_page,
246	            "items": [_serialize_risk_event(e) for e in events],
247	        })
248

250	@blp.route("/blacklist")
251	class BlacklistView(MethodView):
252	    @blp.doc(
253	        summary="Create a blacklist entry (admin only)",
254	        description="Add a user, device, or IP to the blacklist.",
255	        security=[{"BearerAuth": []}],
256	    )
257	    @blp.arguments(BlacklistCreateSchema)
258	    @require_auth
259	    @require_role("admin")
260	    def post(self, data):
261	        from app.models.risk import Blacklist
262	
263	        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
264	
265	        start_at = now
266	        if data.get("start_at"):
267	            try:
268	                start_at = datetime.fromisoformat(data["start_at"])
269	            except ValueError:
270	                pass
271	
272	        end_at = None
273	        if data.get("end_at"):
274	            try:
275	                end_at = datetime.fromisoformat(data["end_at"])
276	            except ValueError:
277	                pass
278	
279	        entry = Blacklist(
280	            target_type=data["target_type"],
281	            target_id=str(data["target_id"]),
282	            reason=data["reason"],
283	            start_at=start_at,
284	            end_at=end_at,
285	            reviewer_id=g.current_user.id,
286	            appeal_status="none",
287	        )
288	        db.session.add(entry)
289	        db.session.flush()
290	
291	        # Create MasterRecord for this blacklist entry
292	        from app.services.master_record_service import create_master_record
293	        create_master_record(
294	            entity_type="blacklist",
295	            entity_id=entry.id,
296	            initial_status="active",
297	            created_by=g.current_user.id,
298	        )
299	
300	        db.session.commit()
301	
302	        return jsonify(_serialize_blacklist(entry)), 201
303	
304	    @blp.doc(
305	        summary="List active blacklist entries (admin only)",
306	        description="Returns blacklist entries that are currently active.",
307	        security=[{"BearerAuth": []}],
308	    )
309	    @require_auth
310	    @require_role("admin")
311	    def get(self):
312	        from app.models.risk import Blacklist
313	
314	        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
315	        entries = (
316	            Blacklist.query
317	            .filter(
318	                Blacklist.start_at <= now,
319	                (Blacklist.end_at == None) | (Blacklist.end_at > now),  # noqa: E711
320	            )
321	            .order_by(Blacklist.created_at.desc())
322	            .all()
323	        )
324	        return jsonify([_serialize_blacklist(e) for e in entries])

1	"""
2	Admin governance API blueprint.
3	
4	Provides:
5	  GET  /admin/users                               – paginated user list
6	  GET  /admin/users/<id>                          – full profile (masked by default)
7	  PATCH /admin/users/<id>                         – update role / status
8	  GET  /admin/audit-logs                          – paginated, filterable
9	  GET  /admin/audit-logs/<id>                     – single entry
10	  GET  /admin/master-records/<entity_type>/<id>   – current record + history
11	  POST /admin/master-records/<entity_type>/<id>/transition – status transition
12	"""
13	import json
14	
15	import flask_smorest
16	import marshmallow as ma
17	from flask import g, jsonify, request
18	from flask.views import MethodView
19

23	    "admin",
24	    "admin",
25	    url_prefix="/admin",
26	    description="Admin governance: users, audit logs, master records",
27	)
28

34	def _get_user_roles(user):
35	    return [r.name for r in user.roles]

39	    """User dict with sensitive fields masked."""
40	    return {
41	        "id": user.id,
42	        "username": user.username,
43	        "email": user.email,
44	        "status": user.status,
45	        "locked_until": user.locked_until.isoformat() if user.locked_until else None,
46	        "roles": _get_user_roles(user),
47	        "phone": "***-***-XXXX",
48	        "address": "[REDACTED]",
49	        "dob": "[REDACTED]",
50	        "created_at": user.created_at.isoformat() if user.created_at else None,
51	        "updated_at": user.updated_at.isoformat() if user.updated_at else None,
52	    }
53	
54	
55	def _user_dict_unmasked(user):
56	    """User dict with sensitive fields decrypted."""
57	    from app.services.encryption_service import decrypt_field
58	    phone = None
59	    address = None
60	    dob = None
61	    try:
62	        if user.phone_encrypted:
63	            phone = decrypt_field(user.phone_encrypted)
64	    except Exception:
65	        phone = None
66	    try:
67	        if user.address_encrypted:
68	            address = decrypt_field(user.address_encrypted)
69	    except Exception:
70	        address = None
71	    try:
72	        if user.dob_encrypted:
73	            dob = decrypt_field(user.dob_encrypted)
74	    except Exception:
75	        dob = None
76	
77	    return {
78	        "id": user.id,
79	        "username": user.username,
80	        "email": user.email,
81	        "status": user.status,
82	        "locked_until": user.locked_until.isoformat() if user.locked_until else None,
83	        "roles": _get_user_roles(user),
84	        "phone": phone,
85	        "address": address,
86	        "dob": dob,
87	        "created_at": user.created_at.isoformat() if user.created_at else None,
88	        "updated_at": user.updated_at.isoformat() if user.updated_at else None,

244	            changes["status"] = {"from": old_status, "to": new_status}
245	
246	        # Update role
247	        new_role_name = patch_data.get("role")
248	        if new_role_name is not None:
249	            role = Role.query.filter_by(name=new_role_name).first()
250	            if role is None:
251	                role = Role(name=new_role_name)
252	                db.session.add(role)
253	                db.session.flush()
254	            existing = UserRole.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id, role_id=role.id).first()
255	            if not existing:
256	                db.session.add(UserRole(user_id=user.id, role_id=role.id))
257	            changes["role_added"] = new_role_name
258	
259	        log_audit(
260	            actor_id=actor.id,
261	            actor_role="admin",
262	            action="admin_update_user",
263	            entity_type="user",
264	            entity_id=id,
265	            detail=changes,
266	            ip=request.remote_addr,
267	        )
268	        db.session.commit()
269	
270	        return jsonify(_user_dict_masked(user)), 200

278	class AdminAuditLogsView(MethodView):

280	        summary="List audit logs, filterable (Admin)",

286	        from app.models.audit import AuditLog
287	        page = request.args.get("page", 1, type=int)
288	        per_page = request.args.get("per_page", 20, type=int)
289	        actor_id = request.args.get("actor_id", type=int)
290	        entity_type = request.args.get("entity_type")
291	        action = request.args.get("action")
292	        date_from = request.args.get("date_from")
293	        date_to = request.args.get("date_to")
294	
295	        query = AuditLog.query
296	        if actor_id is not None:
297	            query = query.filter(AuditLog.actor_id == actor_id)
298	        if entity_type:
299	            query = query.filter(AuditLog.entity_type == entity_type)
300	        if action:
301	            query = query.filter(AuditLog.action == action)
302	        if date_from:
303	            from datetime import datetime
304	            try:
305	                dt = datetime.fromisoformat(date_from)
306	                query = query.filter(AuditLog.created_at >= dt)
307	            except ValueError:
308	                pass
309	        if date_to:
310	            from datetime import datetime
311	            try:
312	                dt = datetime.fromisoformat(date_to)
313	                query = query.filter(AuditLog.created_at <= dt)
314	            except ValueError:
315	                pass
316	
317	        query = query.order_by(AuditLog.created_at.desc())
318	        pagination = query.paginate(page=page, per_page=per_page, error_out=False)
319	
320	        return jsonify({
321	            "items": [_audit_log_dict(e) for e in pagination.items],
322	            "total": pagination.total,
323	            "page": pagination.page,
324	            "per_page": pagination.per_page,
325	            "pages": pagination.pages,
326	        }), 200

366	
367	        history = (

383	        }), 200

395	    def post(self, data, entity_type, entity_id):
396	        from app.services.master_record_service import transition_master_record
397	        from app.services.audit_service import log_audit
398	        from app.extensions import db
399	
400	        actor = g.current_user
401	        to_status = data["to_status"]
402	        reason = data["reason"]
403	
404	        # Build a snapshot of the entity
405	        snapshot = _get_entity_snapshot(entity_type, entity_id)
406	
407	        try:
408	            record = transition_master_record(
409	                entity_type=entity_type,
410	                entity_id=entity_id,
411	                to_status=to_status,
412	                changed_by=actor.id,
413	                reason=reason,
414	                snapshot=snapshot,
415	            )
416	        except ValueError as exc:
417	            return jsonify({"error": "not_found", "message": str(exc)}), 404
418	
419	        log_audit(
420	            actor_id=actor.id,
421	            actor_role="admin",
422	            action="master_record_transition",
423	            entity_type=entity_type,
424	            entity_id=entity_id,
425	            detail={"to_status": to_status, "reason": reason},
426	            ip=request.remote_addr,
427	        )
428	        db.session.commit()
429	
430	        return jsonify({
431	            "id": record.id,
432	            "entity_type": record.entity_type,
433	            "entity_id": record.entity_id,
434	            "current_status": record.current_status,
435	        }), 200

438	def _get_entity_snapshot(entity_type, entity_id):
439	    """Build a snapshot dict for the given entity."""
440	    try:
441	        if entity_type == "user":
442	            from app.models.auth import User
443	            from app.extensions import db
444	            user = db.session.get(User, entity_id)
445	            if user:
446	                return {
447	                    "id": user.id,
448	                    "username": user.username,
449	                    "email": user.email,
450	                    "status": user.status,
451	                    "roles": [r.name for r in user.roles],
452	                }
453	        elif entity_type == "asset":
454	            from app.models.asset import Asset
455	            from app.extensions import db
456	            asset = db.session.get(Asset, entity_id)
457	            if asset:
458	                return {
459	                    "id": asset.id,
460	                    "title": asset.title,
461	                    "asset_type": asset.asset_type,
462	                    "status": getattr(asset, "status", None),
463	                }
464	        elif entity_type == "policy":
465	            from app.models.policy import Policy
466	            from app.extensions import db
467	            policy = db.session.get(Policy, entity_id)
468	            if policy:
469	                return {
470	                    "id": policy.id,
471	                    "name": policy.name,
472	                    "status": policy.status,
473	                    "semver": policy.semver,
474	                }
475	    except Exception:
476	        pass
477	    return {"entity_type": entity_type, "entity_id": entity_id}

74	def _create_user_direct(app, username, email, password):
75	    """Create a user directly in the DB and return a valid session token.
76	
77	    This bypasses the HTTP register/login endpoints so rate-limit state
78	    accumulated by other tests does not interfere with fixture setup.
79	    """
80	    from app.services.auth_service import register_user, login_user
81	
82	    with app.app_context():
83	        # register_user raises ValueError if username/email is already taken
84	        try:
85	            register_user(username=username, email=email, password=password)
86	        except ValueError:
87	            pass  # user already exists from a previous test run in same session
88	
89	        session = login_user(username=username, password=password, ip="127.0.0.1")
90	        return session.token

93	def _make_admin(app, username):
94	    """Promote *username* to admin role (creates role if needed)."""
95	    from app.models.auth import User, Role, UserRole
96	    with app.app_context():
97	        user = User.query.filter_by(username=username).first()
98	        role = Role.query.filter_by(name="admin").first()
99	        if role is None:
100	            role = Role(name="admin")
101	            _db.session.add(role)
102	            _db.session.flush()
103	        existing = UserRole.query.filter_by(user_id=user.id, role_id=role.id).first()
104	        if not existing:
105	            _db.session.add(UserRole(user_id=user.id, role_id=role.id))
106	        _db.session.commit()

21	def _register(client, username=None, email=None, password="ValidPass123!"):
22	    username = username or _unique()
23	    email = email or f"{username}@example.com"
24	    resp = client.post(
25	        "/auth/register",
26	        json={"username": username, "email": email, "password": password},
27	    )

124	def test_login_wrong_password(client):
125	    """Wrong password → 401."""

131	def test_login_lockout_after_five_failures(client):
132	    """5 bad attempts → 6th attempt returns 423 with locked_until."""
133	    _, username, _ = _register(client)
134	
135	    for _ in range(5):
136	        resp = _login(client, username, password="WrongPass123!")
137	        # First five may be 401 (or 423 on the 5th if count hits threshold there)
138	
139	    # The 6th attempt should be 423 (locked)
140	    resp = _login(client, username, password="WrongPass123!")
141	    assert resp.status_code == 423, resp.get_data(as_text=True)
142	    data = resp.get_json()
143	    assert "locked_until" in data
144	
145	
146	def test_login_while_locked(client):
147	    """Login during lock window → 423."""
148	    _, username, _ = _register(client)
149	
150	    # Trigger lockout
151	    for _ in range(5):
152	        _login(client, username, password="WrongPass123!")
153	
154	    # Now try correct password while locked
155	    resp = _login(client, username)
156	    assert resp.status_code == 423, resp.get_data(as_text=True)
157	
158	
159	def test_login_lockout_resets_after_window(client, app):
160	    """4 bad attempts + simulated time past 15-min window → not locked on next attempt."""
161	    _, username, _ = _register(client)
162	
163	    # 4 failures within "now"
164	    for _ in range(4):
165	        _login(client, username, password="WrongPass123!")
166	
167	    # Simulate time advancing past the 15-minute rolling window by patching
168	    # the datetime.now() call inside auth_service so the window query sees
169	    # the old attempts as outside the window.
170	    future_now = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(minutes=16)
171	
172	    with patch(
173	        "app.services.auth_service.datetime",
174	        wraps=__import__("datetime").datetime,
175	    ) as mock_dt:
176	        mock_dt.now.return_value = future_now
177	
178	        resp = _login(client, username)
179	    # With the clock advanced 16 minutes, the 4 old failures are outside the
180	    # 15-minute window, so the failure count is 0 → login succeeds (200) or
181	    # at worst records a new failure (401) without triggering lock (423).
182	    assert resp.status_code != 423, resp.get_data(as_text=True)
183

189	def test_logout_success(client):
190	    """Valid token → 200; same token used again → 401."""
191	    _, username, _ = _register(client)
192	    token = _login(client, username).get_json()["token"]
193	
194	    resp = client.post("/auth/logout", headers=_auth_header(token))
195	    assert resp.status_code == 200, resp.get_data(as_text=True)
196	
197	    # Same token is now revoked → 401
198	    resp2 = client.get("/auth/me", headers=_auth_header(token))
199	    assert resp2.status_code == 401, resp2.get_data(as_text=True)
200	
201	
202	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
203	# Refresh
204	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
205	
206	def test_refresh_token(client):
207	    """Refresh → new token returned; old token rejected afterwards."""
208	    _, username, _ = _register(client)
209	    old_token = _login(client, username).get_json()["token"]
210	
211	    resp = client.post("/auth/refresh", headers=_auth_header(old_token))
212	    assert resp.status_code == 200, resp.get_data(as_text=True)
213	    new_token = resp.get_json()["token"]
214	    assert new_token
215	    assert new_token != old_token
216	
217	    # Old token should be rejected
218	    resp2 = client.get("/auth/me", headers=_auth_header(old_token))
219	    assert resp2.status_code == 401, resp2.get_data(as_text=True)

1	"""
2	Authentication decorators and helpers.
3	"""
4	import functools
5	
6	from flask import request, g, jsonify
7	
8	from app.services.auth_service import get_current_user
9	from app.models.risk import Blacklist
10	from datetime import datetime, timezone
11	
12	
13	def get_token_from_request() -> str | None:
14	    """Extract Bearer token from the Authorization header."""
15	    auth_header = request.headers.get("Authorization", "")
16	    if auth_header.startswith("Bearer "):
17	        return auth_header[len("Bearer "):]
18	    return None
19	
20	
21	def require_auth(f):
22	    """
23	    Decorator that validates the Bearer token and sets g.current_user.
24	
25	    Returns 401 JSON if the token is missing, expired, or revoked.
26	    Returns 403 JSON if the user is on an active blacklist.
27	    """
28	    @functools.wraps(f)
29	    def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
30	        token = get_token_from_request()
31	        if not token:
32	            return jsonify({"error": "unauthorized", "message": "Missing or invalid Authorization header"}), 401
33	
34	        try:
35	            user = get_current_user(token)
36	        except LookupError as exc:
37	            reason = str(exc)
38	            if reason == "token_expired":
39	                return jsonify({"error": "unauthorized", "message": "Session expired"}), 401

112	def require_role(*roles):
113	    """
114	    Decorator factory that ensures g.current_user has at least one of the
115	    given roles. Must be applied *after* require_auth (i.e., listed *before*
116	    it in the decorator stack, which means it wraps the inner function first).
117	
118	    Usage::

120	        @require_auth
121	        @require_role("admin")
122	        def my_view(): ...

125	    or chain as::

128	        class MyView(MethodView):
129	            @require_auth

131	            def post(self, ...):
132	                ...
133	    """

135	        @functools.wraps(f)
136	        def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
137	            user = getattr(g, "current_user", None)
138	            if user is None:
139	                return jsonify({"error": "unauthorized", "message": "Authentication required"}), 401
140	            user_role_names = {r.name for r in user.roles}
141	            if not user_role_names.intersection(roles):
142	                return jsonify({"error": "forbidden", "message": "Insufficient permissions"}), 403
143	            return f(*args, **kwargs)

## Phase 2: Prompt → Code Traceability

### 2.1 List every explicit requirement from the prompt

From `metadata.json`, the prompt specifies:

1. **Authentication**: Local username + password, 12+ char passwords, 5-attempt lockout for 30 min, salted/hashed passwords ✓
2. **Risk Control APIs**: Anomaly signals (rapid account creation, credential stuffing, repeat reserve/hold, coupon cycling, high-velocity profile edits), decisions (allow/challenge/throttle/deny), reason codes ✓
3. **CAPTCHA**: Offline rotating local puzzles + rate limiting ✓
4. **Rate Limiting**: 60 auth/hour/IP, 300 read/min/user, 30 write/min/user ✓
5. **Membership**: Tier definitions, points accrual (1pt/$1 spend), stored-value & points ledgers ✓
6. **Coupons**: Discount, spend-and-save, limited-time, limited-quantity, max 2 stacking, conflict validation ✓
7. **Metadata/Taxonomy**: Categories, tags, keywords, topic/subject, audience, timeliness, source, copyright ✓
8. **Profiles**: Basic info, interest tags, bio, media refs, visibility scopes (public/mutual/custom groups), blocking/hiding ✓
9. **Policy Engine**: Unified rules engine, semantic versioning, effective periods, canary rollout by segment, pre-release validation, one-click rollback ✓
10. **Encryption**: Sensitive fields (phone, address, DOB) encrypted at rest, masked by default, unmasked only to admins with audit annotation ✓
11. **Compliance**: User data export & deletion, deletion = anonymization + 7-year financial retention ✓
12. **Audit Trails**: Full audit logs for admin actions ✓
13. **Database**: SQLite, Flask, SQLAlchemy ✓
14. **Non-Functional**: p95 latency <250ms, deterministic offline, structured logs, no third-party dependencies ✓

| Rate limiting middleware | `/app/extensions.py`, `/app/api/auth.py:117,240` | Uses flask-limiter | ✓ |

content.text:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user's primary request was to break a large MediaVault Asset Governance and Membership system prompt into smaller, feature-based development sub-prompts saved to `.tmp/feature_prompts.md`. Each sub-prompt follows a strict template: Description, Goal, Requirements, Technical Notes, and Expected Output. The file is evaluated using `.tmp/self_test_full.txt`. Throughout the conversation the user made progressively more specific requests: add tests to each prompt, align with the self_test criteria, fill gaps (OLA, global error handler, README in Prompt 1, IDOR tests, complete schema), add Swagger setup, add CI flow with self-sufficient run-tests.sh, update metadata.json and docs/questions.md, update .gitignore, and fix the empty migrations/ directory in the already-implemented repo.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Flask 3.x, SQLAlchemy 2.x, Alembic, Flask-Migrate for the backend
   - flask-smorest + marshmallow for OpenAPI 3.0 / Swagger UI (bundled via swagger-ui-bundle, no CDN)
   - SQLite with WAL mode for single-machine Docker deployment
   - AES-256-GCM field encryption via Python `cryptography` library for phone/address/DOB
   - bcrypt (cost ≥ 12) for password hashing
   - flask-limiter with SQLAlchemyStorage for rate limiting (60 auth/hr/IP, 300 read/min/user, 30 write/min/user)
   - Offline rotating-puzzle CAPTCHA (50+ questions in captcha_puzzles.json)
   - Immutable append-only ledger with idempotency keys
   - Canary policy rollouts via `hash(user_id) % 100 < rollout_pct`
   - Object-level authorization (OLA): `resource.user_id == current_user.id` enforced in service layer
   - IDOR/privilege escalation testing via `test_security_idor.py`
   - Rolling 15-minute lockout window using `login_attempts` table
   - Alembic migrations with DATABASE_URL read from environment
   - Self-sufficient `run-tests.sh` that creates its own `.venv-test/`, installs deps, generates FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY, uses `sqlite:///:memory:`
   - CI flow: `git clone` → `cd repo/` → `docker compose build` → `docker compose up -d` → `./run-tests.sh`
   - python-json-logger for structured JSON logs with RotatingFileHandler
   - pytest + pytest-flask + pytest-cov with ≥80% coverage threshold

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `.tmp/feature_prompts.md`
     - Main deliverable. Contains 12 feature-based prompts with Description, Goal, Requirements, Technical Notes, Tests (table with file:line assertions), and Expected Output sections.
     - All deliverable code scoped to `repo/` subdirectory.
     - Prompt 1 includes complete file tree, all 32 ORM table declarations, global JSON error handler requirement, skeleton README requirement, Swagger/OpenAPI setup (flask-smorest, /openapi.json, /docs, BearerAuth, bundled assets), and "no external runtime calls" constraint.
     - Prompt 2 introduces the OLA rule: "Every endpoint that reads or modifies a resource scoped to a user must verify `resource.user_id == current_user.id`; return 403 if it does not match. Administrators bypass this check. Enforced in service layer."
     - Prompt 12 has `### CI Flow — Deliverables` section specifying exact docker-compose.yml and run-tests.sh content requirements with a table of 8 self-sufficiency steps.
     - Total: 193 tests across 14 test files.

   - `metadata.json`
     - Previously had empty fields. Updated to:
       ```json
       "project_type": "backend-api",
       "frontend_language": "",
       "backend_language": "python",
       "frontend_framework": "",
       "backend_framework": "flask",
       "database": "sqlite"
       ```

   - `docs/questions.md`
     - Was a template with placeholder. Replaced with 12 real business logic questions derived from prompt ambiguities, each with Question, My Understanding, and Solution fields covering: lockout window (rolling), points rounding (total order), coupon stacking scope (submitted codes only), benefit_type conflict (exact enum match), ledger corrections (new debit entries), 7-year retention clock (transaction created_at), canary membership (hash(user_id) % 100), custom group with no members (stub-only), CAPTCHA timing (from attempt 2 on login), risk signal levels (HIGH: stuffing/creation/velocity, MEDIUM: abandon/cycling), master record initial status (user→active, asset/policy→draft, campaign→inactive, blacklist→active), encryption scope (includes audit snapshots and export files).

   - `.gitignore`
     - Was only `.tmp/`. Expanded to cover: Python artifacts (`__pycache__/`, `*.pyc`, `*.egg-info/`), virtual envs (`.venv/`, `.venv-test/`), secrets (`.env`, `*.key`), SQLite WAL files (`repo/data/*.db`, `*.db-shm`, `*.db-wal`), runtime data (`repo/data/logs/`, `repo/data/exports/`), coverage (`htmlcov/`, `.coverage`), pytest cache (`.pytest_cache/`), Alembic bytecode, logs (`*.log`), IDE files (`.vscode/`, `.idea/`, `.DS_Store`). Note: system-reminder shows `settings.local` was added by user/linter.

   - `repo/alembic.ini`
     - New file. Standard Alembic config with `script_location = migrations`. sqlalchemy.url defaults to `sqlite:///data/mediavault.db` but is overridden by DATABASE_URL env var in env.py.

   - `repo/migrations/env.py`
     - New file. Imports `from app.extensions import db` and `import app.models` to register all ORM metadata. Overrides sqlalchemy.url from `DATABASE_URL` env var. Supports both offline and online migration modes.
     ```python
     db_url = os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL", config.get_main_option("sqlalchemy.url"))
     config.set_main_option("sqlalchemy.url", db_url)
     target_metadata = db.metadata
     ```

   - `repo/migrations/script.py.mako`
     - New file. Standard Alembic migration template.

   - `repo/migrations/versions/0001_initial_schema.py`
     - New file. Creates all 32 tables in FK-dependency order with correct indexes and constraints. Includes full `downgrade()` that drops tables in reverse order. Verified working: `alembic upgrade head` against `sqlite:///:memory:` runs cleanly.
     - Tables in order: roles → users → user_roles → sessions → login_attempts → risk_events → blacklists → membership_tiers → memberships → ledgers → campaigns → coupons → coupon_redemptions → taxonomies → dictionaries → assets → download_grants → visibility_groups → visibility_group_members → profiles → profile_follows → profile_blocks → profile_hides → captcha_challenges → captcha_tokens → policies → policy_versions → policy_rollouts → data_requests → master_records → master_record_history → audit_logs
     - Key indexes created: `ix_users_username`, `ix_sessions_token`, `ix_risk_events_user_created`, `ix_risk_events_ip_created`, `ix_ledgers_user_currency`, `ix_coupon_redemptions_user_coupon`, `ix_audit_logs_actor_created`

   - `.tmp/verification-report.md`
     - Created by the Explore agent running the Pre-Submission Verification Agent prompt. Full report covering all 8 phases. Result: PARTIAL PASS. No Blockers, no Highs. Two Warnings: missing `.env.example` and missing project structure section in README.

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Wrong file placement**: Created `run-tests.sh` and `docker-compose.yml` at the repo root instead of inside `repo/`. User corrected: "the run-tests.sh is not created in the root directory only in the repo directory" and "don't generate a code remove run-tests.sh i only need you to generate a prompt." Fixed by deleting the root-level `run-tests.sh` (docker-compose.yml was already gone), and updating feature_prompts.md to specify `repo/` as the deliverable directory.
   - **Code generation instead of prompt generation**: Was creating actual shell script files when user only wanted prompts in feature_prompts.md. User explicitly said "don't generate a code." Fixed by only editing feature_prompts.md going forward.
   - **Alembic migration encoding**: The migration description showed `Initial schema — all tables` with a broken character (—). This was a Unicode rendering issue in the terminal output but does not affect functionality.

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Gap analysis**: Identified 5 gaps between feature_prompts.md and self_test_full.txt criteria (OLA, global error handler, README placement, IDOR tests, incomplete schema). All 5 fixed.
   - **Empty migrations/**: The repo had full application code but migrations/ directory was completely empty. Solved by reading all 10 model files to understand exact schema, then creating alembic.ini, env.py, script.py.mako, and 0001_initial_schema.py with all 32 tables in correct topological order. Verified with `alembic upgrade head`.
   - **Verification result**: The Pre-Submission Verification Agent returned PARTIAL PASS with only two documentation warnings (no blockers, no highs). The two pending fixes are: create `/repo/.env.example` and add project structure section to `/repo/README.md`.

6. All user messages:
   - "prompt A MediaVault Asset Governance and Membership system... Break the main prompt into smaller, feature-based prompts... generate a step by step prompt inside .tmp"

1	# MediaVault API
2	
3	A MediaVault Asset Governance and Membership system backend API.
4	
5	## Quick Start
6	
7	```bash
8	docker compose up
9	```
10	
11	## CI Flow
12	
13	```bash
14	git clone <repo-url>
15	cd repo/
16	docker compose build
17	docker compose up -d
18	./run-tests.sh
19	```
20	
21	Note: `run-tests.sh` is self-sufficient — no prior `pip install` is needed.
22	
23	## Environment Variables
24	
25	| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
26	|----------|----------|---------|-------------|
27	| `FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY` | **Yes** | — | Base64-encoded 32-byte AES key for encrypting sensitive fields. App refuses to start without it. |
28	| `DATABASE_URL` | No | `sqlite:///data/mediavault.db` | SQLAlchemy database URL |
29	| `LOG_LEVEL` | No | `INFO` | Logging level (DEBUG/INFO/WARNING/ERROR/CRITICAL) |
30	| `LOG_FILE` | No | — | Path to log file for rotation |
31	
32	## API Examples
33

35	
36	```bash

38	```
39	
40	Response:

45	### Auth
46	
47	```bash

156	# Submit a deletion request
157	curl -X POST http://localhost:5000/compliance/deletion-request \
158	  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
159	  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" \
160	  -d '{"reason": "right to be forgotten"}'
161	```
162	
163	### Admin
164	
165	```bash
166	# List users (admin)
167	curl http://localhost:5000/admin/users \
168	  -H "Authorization: Bearer <admin-token>"
169	
170	# List audit logs (admin)
171	curl http://localhost:5000/admin/audit-logs \
172	  -H "Authorization: Bearer <admin-token>"
173	```
174	
175	### API Documentation
176	
177	Visit `http://localhost:5000/docs` for the interactive Swagger UI.
178	
179	The OpenAPI spec is available at `http://localhost:5000/openapi.json`.
180	
181	## Known Limitations
182	
183	- Single-machine SQLite deployment; not horizontally scalable.
184	- No JWT; sessions stored in DB — horizontal scaling requires a shared session store.
185	- All operations are offline; no third-party integrations (email, SMS, payment processors).
186	- WAL mode is SQLite-only; switching to PostgreSQL requires removing the WAL pragma setup.
187	- Rate limiting uses in-memory storage by default; counters reset on restart and are not shared across processes.
188	- File uploads are not supported; assets store metadata only, not binary content.
189	- No background job processing; compliance deletion and data export requests must be processed manually or by a separate worker.
190

Both fixes are done:

- **`repo/.env.example`** — created with `FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY`, `DATABASE_URL`, `LOG_LEVEL`, `LOG_FILE`, and `API_VERSION`, including a one-liner to generate the encryption key.
- **`repo/README.md`** — added a "Project Structure" section before "Known Limitations" showing the full `repo/` directory tree with inline descriptions for every subdirectory and key file.

use the prompt inside metadata.json and verify if all features are implemented

1	{
2	    "prompt": "A MediaVault Asset Governance and Membership system with main features including offline account security, asset metadata governance, membership growth and promotions, privacy-safe profiles, a configurable policy engine, and auditable master records. The service exposes resource-oriented API capabilities for Administrators, Moderators/Reviewers, and Regular Users. Authentication supports local username + password only, with passwords hashed and salted and a minimum length of 12 characters; after 5 failed logins in 15 minutes, the account is temporarily locked for 30 minutes. Risk-control APIs support anomaly signals such as rapid account creation, credential stuffing patterns, repeated “reserve/hold” actions without completing checkout, coupon cycling, and high-velocity profile edits; responses include a risk decision (allow, challenge, throttle, or deny) with reason codes. CAPTCHA is implemented as an offline challenge (rotating local puzzle questions) and combined with rate limiting (for example, 60 auth attempts/hour/IP, 300 read requests/minute/user, and 30 write requests/minute/user). Membership and marketing APIs manage tier definitions (e.g., Basic/Silver/Gold), points accrual rules (1 point per $1.00 eligible spend, rounded down), stored-value and points ledgers, and coupon/campaign configuration including discount, spend-and-save, limited-time, and limited-quantity rules; stacking is allowed for at most 2 incentives per order, and conflicts (same benefit type, expired window, exceeded per-user cap) must be rejected at validation time. Metadata/taxonomy APIs maintain multi-level categories, tags, keywords, topic/subject dimensions, audience, timeliness, source, and copyright attributes with dictionary-backed validation and required-field rules per asset type. Profile/privacy APIs support structured profiles (basic info, interest tags, bio, media references) with visibility scopes (public, mutual followers, custom groups), blocking/hiding activity, and download permissions for media marked “restricted.” The backend uses Flask to implement the API capability boundaries and SQLAlchemy with SQLite for local persistence in a single-machine Docker deployment. Core tables include users, roles, sessions, risk_events, blacklists (user/device/IP with start/end, reason, reviewer, appeal status), memberships, ledgers (immutable entries with idempotency keys), campaigns, coupons, coupon_redemptions (unique by user+coupon+order), assets, taxonomies, dictionaries, profiles, visibility_groups, and master_records with status transitions and append-only history snapshots. Policies are unified in a rules engine table set (policy_type enum such as booking/course_selection/warehouse_ops/pricing/risk), with semantic versioning, effective periods, canary rollout percentages by user segment, pre-release validation (schema, conflicts, date windows), and one-click rollback to last-known-good. Sensitive fields (phone, address, DOB) are encrypted at rest using local keys, masked in responses by default, and only returned unmasked to Administrators with an explicit “data_access_purpose” audit annotation. Compliance APIs support user data export and deletion requests, with deletion implemented as irreversible anonymization plus retention of financial/ledger integrity for 7 years. Non-functional requirements include p95 latency under 250 ms for typical reads on a single host, full audit trails for admin actions, deterministic offline operation with no third-party dependencies, and structured logs suitable for local rotation and forensics.",

{"description": "Verify all features from metadata.json prompt are implemented", "subagent_type": "Explore", "prompt": "I need a thorough feature-by-feature verification of this Flask/SQLAlchemy backend codebase against a requirements prompt. The repo is at:

`C:\Users\betea\Documents\projects\eaglepoint\mediavault_asset_governance_membership_backend_api_system\repo\`

The layout:
- `app/api/` — route blueprints (auth.py, membership.py, assets.py, profiles.py, marketing.py, policies.py, risk.py, compliance.py, admin.py, captcha.py, health.py)
- `app/models/` — ORM models (auth.py, membership.py, asset.py, profile.py, marketing.py, policy.py, risk.py, captcha.py, compliance.py, audit.py)
- `app/services/` — business logic
- `app/utils/` — helpers
- `tests/` — pytest suite

For each feature group below, read the relevant source files and confirm whether it is implemented (PASS), partially implemented (PARTIAL), or missing (FAIL). Be specific: cite file + line number for each finding.

---

**Feature groups to verify:**

**F1 — Authentication**
- Local username+password login only
- Passwords bcrypt-hashed, minimum 12 chars enforced
- After 5 failed logins within 15 minutes → account locked for 30 minutes
- Session token generation and validation
- Logout invalidates token

**F2 — Risk Control**
- `/risk/evaluate` endpoint exists
- Anomaly signals detected: rapid account creation, credential stuffing, reserve/hold without checkout, coupon cycling, high-velocity profile edits
- Risk decision output: allow / challenge / throttle / deny with reason codes

**F3 — CAPTCHA & Rate Limiting**
- Offline rotating puzzle questions (captcha_puzzles.json or similar)
- CAPTCHA challenge + verify endpoints
- Rate limits: 60 auth/hr/IP, 300 read/min/user, 30 write/min/user
- CAPTCHA gate on login (from attempt 2+)

**F4 — Membership & Marketing**
- Tier definitions (Basic / Silver / Gold)
- Points accrual: 1 pt per $1.00 eligible spend, rounded down
- Immutable ledger with idempotency keys
- Coupon types: discount, spend-and-save, limited-time, limited-quantity
- Stacking: max 2 incentives per order
- Conflict rejection: same benefit_type, expired window, exceeded per-user cap

**F5 — Metadata / Taxonomy**
- Multi-level categories (taxonomies table)
- Tags, keywords, topic/subject, audience, timeliness, source, copyright attributes on assets
- Dictionary-backed validation (dictionaries table)
- Required-field rules per asset type

**F6 — Profiles & Privacy**
- Structured profiles (basic info, interest tags, bio, media references)
- Visibility scopes: public, mutual_followers, custom_groups
- Blocking and hiding activity
- Download permissions for "restricted" media (download_grants)

**F7 — ORM Tables**
Confirm all 32 tables exist in models: users, roles, user_roles, sessions, login_attempts, risk_events, blacklists, membership_tiers, memberships, ledgers, campaigns, coupons, coupon_redemptions, taxonomies, dictionaries, assets, download_grants, visibility_groups, visibility_group_members, profiles, profile_follows, profile_blocks, profile_hides, captcha_challenges, captcha_tokens, policies, policy_versions, policy_rollouts, data_requests, master_records, master_record_history, audit_logs

**F8 — Policy Engine**
- Policy types enum (booking/course_selection/warehouse_ops/pricing/risk)
- Semantic versioning
- Effective periods (effective_from / effective_to)
- Canary rollout by user segment: `hash(user_id) % 100 < rollout_pct`
- Pre-release validation (schema check, conflict check, date windows)
- One-click rollback to last-known-good

**F9 — Encryption**
- phone, address, DOB encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM or equivalent)
- Masked by default in API responses
- Unmasked only for Admins with `data_access_purpose` audit annotation

**F10 — Compliance**
- `/compliance/export-request` endpoint
- `/compliance/deletion-request` endpoint
- Deletion = irreversible anonymization
- Financial/ledger records retained for 7 years

**F11 — Non-functional**
- Full audit trail for admin actions (audit_logs table + service)
- Structured JSON logs (python-json-logger or equivalent)
- No CDN / third-party runtime calls (offline-only)
- p95 latency test (performance test exists)
- Health check endpoint `/healthz`

**F12 — Admin**
- Admin-only endpoints: list users, list audit logs, master records
- Object-level authorization (OLA): `resource.user_id == current_user.id`, 403 otherwise
- IDOR tests exist in `tests/test_security_idor.py`

---

Output a markdown table with columns: Feature Group | Status (PASS/PARTIAL/FAIL) | Evidence (file:line or "missing") | Notes

Then give a short summary paragraph of overall status."}

2	Authentication & account security business logic.
3	"""
4	import secrets
5	from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
6	
7	from passlib.context import CryptContext
8	from sqlalchemy.exc import IntegrityError
9	
10	from app.extensions import db
11	from app.models.auth import User, Role, UserRole, Session, LoginAttempt
12	from app.models.profile import Profile

14	from app.models.risk import Blacklist
15	from app.services.audit_service import log_audit
16	from app.services.master_record_service import create_master_record

18	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
19	# Password hashing
20	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
21	_pwd_context = CryptContext(schemes=["bcrypt"], bcrypt__rounds=12, deprecated="auto")
22	
23	LOCKOUT_ATTEMPTS = 5
24	LOCKOUT_WINDOW_MINUTES = 15
25	LOCKOUT_DURATION_MINUTES = 30
26	SESSION_TTL_HOURS = 24
27	
28	
29	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
30	# Internal helpers
31	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
32	
33	def _hash_password(plain: str) -> str:
34	    return _pwd_context.hash(plain)
35	
36	
37	def _verify_password(plain: str, hashed: str) -> bool:
38	    return _pwd_context.verify(plain, hashed)
39	
40	
41	def _count_recent_failures(user_id: int, now: datetime) -> int:
42	    """Count failed login attempts in the rolling 15-minute window."""
43	    window_start = now - timedelta(minutes=LOCKOUT_WINDOW_MINUTES)
44	    return (
45	        LoginAttempt.query
46	        .filter(
47	            LoginAttempt.user_id == user_id,
48	            LoginAttempt.success == False,  # noqa: E712
49	            LoginAttempt.attempted_at >= window_start,
50	        )
51	        .count()
52	    )
53	
54	
55	def _record_login_attempt(user_id, ip: str, success: bool) -> None:
56	    attempt = LoginAttempt(
57	        user_id=user_id,
58	        ip=ip,
59	        attempted_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
60	        success=success,
61	    )
62	    db.session.add(attempt)
63	
64	
65	def _is_user_blacklisted(user_id: int) -> bool:
66	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
67	    return (
68	        Blacklist.query
69	        .filter(
70	            Blacklist.target_type == "user",
71	            Blacklist.target_id == str(user_id),
72	            Blacklist.start_at <= now,
73	            db.or_(Blacklist.end_at == None, Blacklist.end_at > now),  # noqa: E711
74	        )
75	        .first() is not None
76	    )
77	
78	
79	def _get_or_create_role(name: str) -> Role:
80	    role = Role.query.filter_by(name=name).first()
81	    if role is None:
82	        role = Role(name=name)
83	        db.session.add(role)
84	        db.session.flush()
85	    return role

92	def register_user(username: str, email: str, password: str) -> User:
93	    """
94	    Register a new user.
95	
96	    Creates User, Profile, Membership, and MasterRecord in one transaction.
97	    Assigns the "user" role.
98	
99	    :raises ValueError: on duplicate username / email (caller should map to 409)
100	    """
101	    password_hash = _hash_password(password)
102	
103	    user = User(
104	        username=username,
105	        email=email,
106	        password_hash=password_hash,
107	        status="active",
108	        created_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
109	        updated_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
110	    )
111	    db.session.add(user)
112	    try:
113	        db.session.flush()  # catch unique violations early
114	    except IntegrityError:
115	        db.session.rollback()
116	        raise ValueError("username_or_email_taken")
117	
118	    # Assign "user" role
119	    user_role = _get_or_create_role("user")
120	    db.session.add(UserRole(user_id=user.id, role_id=user_role.id))
121	
122	    # Create profile
123	    profile = Profile(
124	        user_id=user.id,
125	        display_name=username,
126	        visibility_scope="public",
127	        created_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
128	        updated_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
129	    )
130	    db.session.add(profile)
131	
132	    # Create membership (no tier yet)
133	    membership = Membership(
134	        user_id=user.id,
135	        points_balance=0,
136	        stored_value_balance=0,
137	        created_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
138	        updated_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc),
139	    )
140	    db.session.add(membership)
141	
142	    db.session.flush()
143	
144	    # Create master record for this user
145	    create_master_record(
146	        entity_type="user",
147	        entity_id=user.id,
148	        initial_status="active",
149	        created_by=user.id,
150	    )
151	
152	    db.session.commit()
153	    return user
154	
155	
156	def login_user(username: str, password: str, ip: str) -> Session:
157	    """
158	    Authenticate a user and create a session.
159	
160	    :raises LookupError:    if the username does not exist
161	    :raises PermissionError: if the account is locked
162	    :raises ValueError:     if the password is wrong
163	    :returns:               a valid Session object
164	    """
165	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
166	
167	    user = User.query.filter_by(username=username).first()
168	    if user is None:
169	        # Don't reveal whether the username exists
170	        raise LookupError("invalid_credentials")
171	
172	    # Check if user is anonymized
173	    if user.status == 'anonymized':
174	        raise ValueError("account_anonymized")
175	
176	    # Check lock (DB-level locked_until field)
177	    if user.locked_until is not None:
178	        locked_until_aware = user.locked_until
179	        if locked_until_aware.tzinfo is None:
180	            locked_until_aware = locked_until_aware.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
181	        if locked_until_aware > now:
182	            log_audit(
183	                actor_id=user.id,
184	                actor_role=None,
185	                action="login_failure",
186	                entity_type="user",
187	                entity_id=user.id,
188	                detail={"reason": "account_locked", "ip": ip},
189	                ip=ip,
190	            )
191	            db.session.commit()
192	            raise PermissionError(f"account_locked:{user.locked_until.isoformat()}")
193	
194	    # Count recent failures (rolling window) BEFORE verifying password
195	    recent_failures = _count_recent_failures(user.id, now)
196	
197	    # Verify password
198	    if not _verify_password(password, user.password_hash):
199	        _record_login_attempt(user.id, ip, success=False)
200	        db.session.flush()
201	
202	        # Re-count after recording this failure
203	        new_failure_count = _count_recent_failures(user.id, now)
204	
205	        log_audit(
206	            actor_id=user.id,
207	            actor_role=None,
208	            action="login_failure",
209	            entity_type="user",
210	            entity_id=user.id,
211	            detail={"reason": "wrong_password", "ip": ip, "failure_count": new_failure_count},
212	            ip=ip,
213	        )
214	
215	        if new_failure_count >= LOCKOUT_ATTEMPTS:
216	            locked_until = now + timedelta(minutes=LOCKOUT_DURATION_MINUTES)
217	            user.locked_until = locked_until
218	            user.status = "locked"
219	            log_audit(
220	                actor_id=user.id,
221	                actor_role=None,
222	                action="account_locked",
223	                entity_type="user",
224	                entity_id=user.id,
225	                detail={"locked_until": locked_until.isoformat(), "ip": ip},
226	                ip=ip,
227	            )
228	            db.session.commit()
229	            raise PermissionError(f"account_locked:{locked_until.isoformat()}")
230	
231	        db.session.commit()
232	        raise ValueError("invalid_credentials")
233	
234	    # Password is correct — clear any stale lock and create session
235	    if user.locked_until is not None:
236	        user.locked_until = None
237	        user.status = "active"
238	
239	    _record_login_attempt(user.id, ip, success=True)
240	
241	    token = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
242	    expires_at = now + timedelta(hours=SESSION_TTL_HOURS)
243	    session = Session(
244	        user_id=user.id,
245	        token=token,
246	        expires_at=expires_at,
247	        created_at=now,
248	    )
249	    db.session.add(session)
250	
251	    log_audit(
252	        actor_id=user.id,
253	        actor_role=None,
254	        action="login_success",
255	        entity_type="user",
256	        entity_id=user.id,
257	        detail={"ip": ip},
258	        ip=ip,
259	    )
260	
261	    db.session.commit()
262	    return session
263	
264	
265	def logout_user(token: str) -> None:
266	    """
267	    Revoke the session identified by *token*.
268	
269	    :raises LookupError: if the token is not found or already revoked
270	    """
271	    session = Session.query.filter_by(token=token).first()
272	    if session is None or session.revoked_at is not None:
273	        raise LookupError("invalid_token")
274	
275	    session.revoked_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
276	    db.session.commit()
277	
278	
279	def refresh_session(token: str) -> Session:
280	    """
281	    Create a new session token and revoke the old one.
282	
283	    :raises LookupError:  if token not found, revoked, or expired
284	    :returns:             the new Session
285	    """
286	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
287	    old_session = Session.query.filter_by(token=token).first()
288	    if old_session is None:
289	        raise LookupError("invalid_token")
290	    if old_session.revoked_at is not None:
291	        raise LookupError("token_revoked")
292	
293	    expires_at = old_session.expires_at
294	    if expires_at.tzinfo is None:
295	        expires_at = expires_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
296	    if expires_at <= now:
297	        raise LookupError("token_expired")
298	
299	    # Revoke old token
300	    old_session.revoked_at = now
301	
302	    # Create new session
303	    new_token = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
304	    new_expires = now + timedelta(hours=SESSION_TTL_HOURS)
305	    new_session = Session(
306	        user_id=old_session.user_id,
307	        token=new_token,
308	        expires_at=new_expires,
309	        created_at=now,
310	    )
311	    db.session.add(new_session)
312	    db.session.commit()
313	    return new_session
314	
315	
316	def get_current_user(token: str) -> User:
317	    """
318	    Return the User for a valid, non-expired, non-revoked session token.
319	
320	    :raises LookupError: for any invalid condition
321	    """
322	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
323	    session = Session.query.filter_by(token=token).first()
324	    if session is None:
325	        raise LookupError("invalid_token")
326	    if session.revoked_at is not None:
327	        raise LookupError("token_revoked")
328	
329	    expires_at = session.expires_at
330	    if expires_at.tzinfo is None:
331	        expires_at = expires_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
332	    if expires_at <= now:
333	        raise LookupError("token_expired")
334	
335	    user = db.session.get(User, session.user_id)
336	    if user is None:
337	        raise LookupError("user_not_found")
338	
339	    return user
340	
341	
342	def unlock_user(user_id: int, admin_id: int) -> User:
343	    """
344	    Clear the lockout on a user (admin action).
345	
346	    :raises LookupError: if the user does not exist
347	    """
348	    user = db.session.get(User, user_id)
349	    if user is None:
350	        raise LookupError("user_not_found")
351	
352	    user.locked_until = None
353	    user.status = "active"
354	
355	    log_audit(
356	        actor_id=admin_id,
357	        actor_role="admin",
358	        action="account_unlocked",
359	        entity_type="user",
360	        entity_id=user_id,
361	        detail={"unlocked_by": admin_id},
362	        ip=None,
363	    )
364	
365	    db.session.commit()
366	    return user

1	"""
2	Authentication API blueprint.
3	"""
4	import flask_smorest
5	import marshmallow as ma
6	from marshmallow import validate
7	from flask import request, g, jsonify
8	from flask.views import MethodView
9	from werkzeug.exceptions import abort as http_abort
10	
11	from flask_limiter.util import get_remote_address

13	from app.utils.auth_utils import require_auth, require_role

16	
17	
18	def _get_user_key():
19	    """Rate-limit key: user-id extracted from Bearer token, else remote IP."""
20	    auth_header = request.headers.get("Authorization", "")
21	    if auth_header.startswith("Bearer "):
22	        token_str = auth_header[7:]
23	        # Try to resolve user id from session token
24	        try:
25	            from app.models.auth import Session
26	            session = Session.query.filter_by(token=token_str, is_active=True).first()
27	            if session:
28	                return f"user:{session.user_id}"
29	        except Exception:
30	            pass
31	    return get_remote_address()
32	
33	blp = flask_smorest.Blueprint(
34	    "auth",
35	    "auth",
36	    url_prefix="/auth",
37	    description="Authentication & account security",
38	)
39	
40	
41	def _abort(status_code, message):
42	    """Return a JSON error response (bypasses smorest schema serialization)."""
43	    from flask import current_app
44	    resp = jsonify({"error": str(status_code), "message": message})
45	    resp.status_code = status_code
46	    # Raise an HTTPException so Flask's error-handler chain sees it.
47	    http_abort(resp)

54	class RegisterSchema(ma.Schema):
55	    username = ma.fields.Str(
56	        required=True,
57	        validate=validate.Regexp(
58	            r"^[a-zA-Z0-9_]{3,64}$",
59	            error="Username must be 3-64 characters and contain only letters, digits, or underscores.",
60	        ),
61	    )
62	    email = ma.fields.Email(required=True)

64	        required=True,

66	        load_only=True,

105	@blp.route("/register")
106	class RegisterView(MethodView):
107	    @blp.doc(
108	        summary="Register a new user",
109	        description=(
110	            "Creates a new user account along with a Profile, Membership, and MasterRecord. "
111	            "Returns 409 on duplicate username or email, 422 on validation failure. "
112	            "Requires a valid X-Captcha-Token header (skipped in TESTING mode)."
113	        ),
114	    )
115	    @blp.arguments(RegisterSchema)
116	    @blp.response(201, RegisterResponseSchema)
117	    @limiter.limit("60/hour")
118	    @require_captcha_token
119	    def post(self, data):
120	        from app.services.auth_service import register_user
121	
122	        try:
123	            user = register_user(
124	                username=data["username"],
125	                email=data["email"],
126	                password=data["password"],
127	            )
128	        except ValueError as exc:
129	            if "taken" in str(exc):
130	                return jsonify({"error": "conflict", "message": "Username or email already in use."}), 409
131	            return jsonify({"error": "unprocessable_entity", "message": str(exc)}), 422
132	
133	        return {"user_id": user.id, "username": user.username, "email": user.email}, 201

138	    @blp.doc(
139	        summary="Login",
140	        description=(
141	            "Authenticate with username and password. "
142	            "Returns 401 on wrong password, 423 when the account is locked."
143	        ),
144	    )
145	    @blp.arguments(LoginSchema)
146	    @blp.response(200, TokenResponseSchema)
147	    @limiter.limit("60/hour")
148	    def post(self, data):
149	        from app.services.auth_service import login_user
150	
151	        ip = request.remote_addr or "unknown"
152	
153	        try:
154	            session = login_user(
155	                username=data["username"],
156	                password=data["password"],
157	                ip=ip,
158	            )
159	        except ValueError as exc:
160	            if "account_anonymized" in str(exc):
161	                return jsonify({"error": "account_anonymized", "message": "This account has been deleted."}), 401
162	            return jsonify({"error": "unauthorized", "message": "Invalid username or password."}), 401
163	        except LookupError:
164	            return jsonify({"error": "unauthorized", "message": "Invalid username or password."}), 401
165	        except PermissionError as exc:
166	            msg = str(exc)
167	            if msg.startswith("account_locked:"):
168	                locked_until = msg.split(":", 1)[1]
169	                return jsonify({
170	                    "error": "locked",
171	                    "message": "Account is locked due to too many failed login attempts.",
172	                    "locked_until": locked_until,
173	                }), 423
174	            return jsonify({"error": "forbidden", "message": "Account access denied."}), 403
175	
176	        return {
177	            "token": session.token,
178	            "expires_at": session.expires_at.isoformat(),
179	            "user_id": session.user_id,
180	        }

1	"""
2	Risk evaluation service for MediaVault.
3	
4	Evaluates risk signals from DB state and returns a decision + reasons.
5	"""
6	from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
7	import json
8	
9	from sqlalchemy import func
10	
11	from app.extensions import db
12	from app.models.risk import RiskEvent, Blacklist
13	from app.models.auth import LoginAttempt
14	
15	
16	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
17	# Default thresholds
18	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
19	THRESHOLDS = {
20	    "rapid_account_creation": {"count": 3, "window_minutes": 10, "severity": "HIGH"},
21	    "credential_stuffing":    {"count": 10, "window_minutes": 5,  "severity": "HIGH"},
22	    "reserve_abandon":        {"count": 4, "window_minutes": 60,  "severity": "MEDIUM"},
23	    "coupon_cycling":         {"count": 3, "window_minutes": 1440, "severity": "MEDIUM"},
24	    "high_velocity_profile_edit": {"count": 5, "window_minutes": 10, "severity": "HIGH"},
25	}
26	
27	
28	def _get_thresholds():
29	    """
30	    Return thresholds dict. Reads from active Policy if one exists, else returns
31	    hardcoded defaults. This keeps the design open for future policy overrides.
32	    """
33	    try:
34	        from app.models.policy import Policy, PolicyVersion
35	        policy = Policy.query.filter_by(name="risk_thresholds", status="active").first()
36	        if policy:
37	            version = (
38	                PolicyVersion.query
39	                .filter_by(policy_id=policy.id)
40	                .order_by(PolicyVersion.version.desc())
41	                .first()
42	            )
43	            if version and version.config_json:
44	                config = json.loads(version.config_json)
45	                if isinstance(config, dict) and "thresholds" in config:
46	                    return config["thresholds"]
47	    except Exception:
48	        pass
49	    return THRESHOLDS
50	
51	
52	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	# Individual signal evaluators
54	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
55	
56	def _check_rapid_account_creation(ip, thresholds):
57	    if not ip:
58	        return False
59	    cfg = thresholds.get("rapid_account_creation", THRESHOLDS["rapid_account_creation"])
60	    window_start = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=cfg["window_minutes"])
61	    count = db.session.query(func.count(RiskEvent.id)).filter(
62	        RiskEvent.event_type == "registration",
63	        RiskEvent.ip == ip,
64	        RiskEvent.created_at >= window_start,
65	    ).scalar() or 0
66	    return count >= cfg["count"]
67	
68	
69	def _check_credential_stuffing(ip, thresholds):
70	    if not ip:
71	        return False
72	    cfg = thresholds.get("credential_stuffing", THRESHOLDS["credential_stuffing"])
73	    window_start = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=cfg["window_minutes"])
74	    distinct_users = db.session.query(
75	        func.count(func.distinct(LoginAttempt.user_id))
76	    ).filter(
77	        LoginAttempt.ip == ip,
78	        LoginAttempt.success == False,  # noqa: E712
79	        LoginAttempt.attempted_at >= window_start,
80	    ).scalar() or 0
81	    return distinct_users >= cfg["count"]
82	
83	
84	def _check_reserve_abandon(user_id, thresholds):
85	    if not user_id:
86	        return False
87	    cfg = thresholds.get("reserve_abandon", THRESHOLDS["reserve_abandon"])
88	    window_start = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=cfg["window_minutes"])
89	    reserve_count = db.session.query(func.count(RiskEvent.id)).filter(
90	        RiskEvent.event_type == "reserve",
91	        RiskEvent.user_id == user_id,
92	        RiskEvent.created_at >= window_start,
93	    ).scalar() or 0
94	    checkout_count = db.session.query(func.count(RiskEvent.id)).filter(
95	        RiskEvent.event_type == "checkout",
96	        RiskEvent.user_id == user_id,
97	        RiskEvent.created_at >= window_start,
98	    ).scalar() or 0
99	    return (reserve_count - checkout_count) >= cfg["count"]
100	
101	
102	def _check_coupon_cycling(user_id, thresholds):
103	    if not user_id:
104	        return False
105	    cfg = thresholds.get("coupon_cycling", THRESHOLDS["coupon_cycling"])
106	    window_start = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=cfg["window_minutes"])
107	    redeem_count = db.session.query(func.count(RiskEvent.id)).filter(
108	        RiskEvent.event_type == "coupon_redeem",
109	        RiskEvent.user_id == user_id,
110	        RiskEvent.created_at >= window_start,
111	    ).scalar() or 0
112	    refund_count = db.session.query(func.count(RiskEvent.id)).filter(
113	        RiskEvent.event_type == "coupon_refund",
114	        RiskEvent.user_id == user_id,
115	        RiskEvent.created_at >= window_start,
116	    ).scalar() or 0
117	    # Flag when there are >= threshold of both types (paired cycles)
118	    return redeem_count >= cfg["count"] and refund_count >= cfg["count"]
119	
120	
121	def _check_high_velocity_profile_edit(user_id, thresholds):
122	    if not user_id:
123	        return False
124	    cfg = thresholds.get("high_velocity_profile_edit", THRESHOLDS["high_velocity_profile_edit"])
125	    window_start = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(minutes=cfg["window_minutes"])
126	    count = db.session.query(func.count(RiskEvent.id)).filter(
127	        RiskEvent.event_type == "profile_edit",
128	        RiskEvent.user_id == user_id,
129	        RiskEvent.created_at >= window_start,
130	    ).scalar() or 0
131	    return count >= cfg["count"]
132	
133	
134	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
135	# Main evaluation function
136	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
137	
138	def evaluate_risk(event_type, ip, user_id=None, device_id=None, metadata=None):
139	    """
140	    Evaluate risk signals and return decision + reasons.
141	
142	    Decision logic (first match wins):
143	    - Any HIGH signal fires  -> 'deny'
144	    - Any MEDIUM signal fires -> 'throttle'
145	    - Otherwise              -> 'allow'
146	
147	    A RiskEvent row is always persisted after evaluation.
148	
149	    Returns: {'decision': str, 'reasons': list}
150	    """
151	    thresholds = _get_thresholds()
152	
153	    fired_high = []
154	    fired_medium = []
155	
156	    # --- HIGH severity checks ---
157	    if _check_rapid_account_creation(ip, thresholds):
158	        fired_high.append("rapid_account_creation")
159	
160	    if _check_credential_stuffing(ip, thresholds):
161	        fired_high.append("credential_stuffing")
162	
163	    if _check_high_velocity_profile_edit(user_id, thresholds):
164	        fired_high.append("high_velocity_profile_edit")
165	
166	    # --- MEDIUM severity checks ---
167	    if _check_reserve_abandon(user_id, thresholds):
168	        fired_medium.append("reserve_abandon")
169	
170	    if _check_coupon_cycling(user_id, thresholds):
171	        fired_medium.append("coupon_cycling")
172	
173	    # --- Decision ---
174	    if fired_high:
175	        decision = "deny"
176	        reasons = fired_high + fired_medium
177	    elif fired_medium:
178	        decision = "throttle"
179	        reasons = fired_medium
180	    else:
181	        decision = "allow"
182	        reasons = []
183	
184	    # --- Persist the event ---
185	    event = RiskEvent(
186	        event_type=event_type,
187	        user_id=user_id,
188	        ip=ip,
189	        device_id=device_id,
190	        decision=decision,
191	        reasons=json.dumps(reasons),
192	        metadata_json=json.dumps(metadata) if metadata else None,
193	    )
194	    db.session.add(event)
195	    db.session.commit()
196	
197	    return {"decision": decision, "reasons": reasons}
198

134	def _check_ip_blacklist(ip):
135	    """Return True if the given IP is actively blacklisted."""
136	    if not ip:
137	        return False
138	    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
139	    entry = (
140	        db.session.query(__import__("app.models.risk", fromlist=["Blacklist"]).Blacklist)
141	        .filter(
142	            __import__("app.models.risk", fromlist=["Blacklist"]).Blacklist.target_type == "ip",
143	            __import__("app.models.risk", fromlist=["Blacklist"]).Blacklist.target_id == ip,
144	            __import__("app.models.risk", fromlist=["Blacklist"]).Blacklist.start_at <= now,
145	            (
146	                __import__("app.models.risk", fromlist=["Blacklist"]).Blacklist.end_at == None  # noqa: E711
147	            ) | (
148	                __import__("app.models.risk", fromlist=["Blacklist"]).Blacklist.end_at > now
149	            ),
150	        )
151	        .first()
152	    )
153	    return entry is not None

160	@blp.route("/evaluate")
161	class EvaluateView(MethodView):
162	    @blp.doc(
163	        summary="Evaluate risk for an event",
164	        description="Submit an event for risk evaluation. Returns decision and triggered signals.",
165	        security=[{"BearerAuth": []}],
166	    )
167	    @blp.arguments(EvaluateRequestSchema)
168	    @blp.response(200, EvaluateResponseSchema)
169	    @require_auth
170	    def post(self, data):
171	        from app.services.risk_service import evaluate_risk
172	
173	        ip = data.get("ip") or request.remote_addr or "unknown"
174	
175	        # Check IP blacklist before evaluation
176	        from app.models.risk import Blacklist
177	        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
178	        ip_blacklisted = (
179	            Blacklist.query
180	            .filter(
181	                Blacklist.target_type == "ip",
182	                Blacklist.target_id == ip,
183	                Blacklist.start_at <= now,
184	                (Blacklist.end_at == None) | (Blacklist.end_at > now),  # noqa: E711
185	            )
186	            .first()
187	        )
188	        if ip_blacklisted:
189	            return jsonify({"error": "forbidden", "message": "IP address is blacklisted"}), 403
190	
191	        result = evaluate_risk(
192	            event_type=data["event_type"],
193	            ip=ip,
194	            user_id=data.get("user_id"),
195	            device_id=data.get("device_id"),
196	            metadata=data.get("metadata"),
197	        )
198	        return result

201	@blp.route("/events")
202	class RiskEventsView(MethodView):
203	    @blp.doc(
204	        summary="List risk events (admin only)",
205	        description="Paginated, filterable list of risk events.",
206	        security=[{"BearerAuth": []}],
207	    )
208	    @blp.arguments(RiskEventsQuerySchema, location="query")
209	    @require_auth
210	    @require_role("admin")
211	    def get(self, query):
212	        from app.models.risk import RiskEvent
213	
214	        q = db.session.query(RiskEvent)
215	
216	        if query.get("user_id"):
217	            q = q.filter(RiskEvent.user_id == query["user_id"])
218	        if query.get("ip"):
219	            q = q.filter(RiskEvent.ip == query["ip"])
220	        if query.get("event_type"):
221	            q = q.filter(RiskEvent.event_type == query["event_type"])
222	        if query.get("decision"):
223	            q = q.filter(RiskEvent.decision == query["decision"])
224	        if query.get("date_from"):
225	            try:
226	                dt = datetime.fromisoformat(query["date_from"])
227	                q = q.filter(RiskEvent.created_at >= dt)
228	            except ValueError:
229	                pass
230	        if query.get("date_to"):
231	            try:
232	                dt = datetime.fromisoformat(query["date_to"])
233	                q = q.filter(RiskEvent.created_at <= dt)
234	            except ValueError:
235	                pass
236	
237	        page = max(1, query.get("page", 1))
238	        per_page = min(100, max(1, query.get("per_page", 20)))
239	        total = q.count()
240	        events = q.order_by(RiskEvent.created_at.desc()).offset((page - 1) * per_page).limit(per_page).all()
241	
242	        return jsonify({
243	            "total": total,
244	            "page": page,
245	            "per_page": per_page,
246	            "items": [_serialize_risk_event(e) for e in events],
247	        })

2	CAPTCHA API blueprint.

4	import flask_smorest

6	from flask import jsonify
7	from flask.views import MethodView
8	
9	blp = flask_smorest.Blueprint(
10	    "captcha",

12	    url_prefix="/captcha",
13	    description="Offline CAPTCHA challenge & verification",
14	)
15	
16	
17	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
18	# Schemas
19	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
20

22	    challenge_id = ma.fields.Int()
23	    question = ma.fields.Str()
24	
25	
26	class VerifySchema(ma.Schema):
27	    challenge_id = ma.fields.Int(required=True)
28	    answer = ma.fields.Str(required=True)
29	
30	
31	class VerifyResponseSchema(ma.Schema):
32	    valid = ma.fields.Bool()
33	    token = ma.fields.Str(load_default=None, dump_default=None)
34	    error = ma.fields.Str(load_default=None, dump_default=None)
35	
36	
37	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
38	# Routes
39	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
40	
41	@blp.route("/challenge")
42	class ChallengeView(MethodView):
43	    @blp.doc(
44	        summary="Get a CAPTCHA challenge",
45	        description="Returns a challenge_id and a question the user must answer.",
46	    )
47	    @blp.response(200, ChallengeResponseSchema)
48	    def get(self):
49	        from app.services.captcha_service import create_challenge
50	        return create_challenge()
51	
52	
53	@blp.route("/verify")
54	class VerifyView(MethodView):
55	    @blp.doc(
56	        summary="Verify a CAPTCHA answer",
57	        description=(
58	            "Submit an answer for a challenge. Returns valid=true and a one-time token "
59	            "on success. Returns an error code if expired or max attempts exceeded."
60	        ),
61	    )
62	    @blp.arguments(VerifySchema)
63	    @blp.response(200, VerifyResponseSchema)
64	    def post(self, data):
65	        from app.services.captcha_service import verify_challenge
66	
67	        result = verify_challenge(data["challenge_id"], data["answer"])
68	
69	        error_code = result.get("error")
70	        if error_code == "challenge_expired":
71	            return jsonify({"error": "challenge_expired", "message": "Challenge has expired."}), 400
72	        if error_code == "max_attempts_exceeded":
73	            return jsonify({"error": "max_attempts_exceeded", "message": "Too many attempts."}), 400
74	        if error_code == "challenge_not_found":
75	            return jsonify({"error": "not_found", "message": "Challenge not found."}), 404
76	
77	        return result
